story_clusters.headline: Tamil Nadu Election Exit Poll 2026: ‘Wait for 3 more days… NDA will form next government,’ says BJP leader Murugan
story_clusters.summary: Exit polls for the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections suggest the NDA will form the next government, according to a BJP leader. In Kerala, Minister P A Mohamed Riyas expressed confidence in the CPI(M)-led LDF ahead of the assembly election results. Security has been tightened in sensitive areas of Kerala ahead of the poll verdict.

story_clusters.summary: President Trump announced that the U.S. Navy will assist ships trapped in the Strait of Hormuz, following what he described as "very positive" talks with Iran. The move coincides with reports that the U.S. plans to maintain its naval blockade on Iran, contributing to gains in aluminum prices. The initiative has been dubbed "Project Freedom" by the administration.

story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that tensions between the US and Iran have intensified, with the UK preparing contingency plans for fuel supplies amid concerns over potential disruptions. US Treasury Secretary Bessent stated that Iran is facing severe economic pressure, while US President Trump indicated the US would assist in freeing ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. Additionally, crude oil prices have fluctuated due to the ongoing uncertainty surrounding the conflict.

story_clusters.summary: Assembly elections in 2026 are underway with counting scheduled, and the Election Commission of India (ECI) has emphasized transparency in the process. Early trends in Kerala indicate the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) leading over the Left Democratic Front (LDF), while exit polls project the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) likely to retain power in Assam under Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. In West Bengal, vote counting has begun amid questions over whether the Trinamool Congress (TMC) can maintain its dominance against the BJP challenge.

story_clusters.summary: The Election Commission (EC) has announced that no victory rallies will be allowed in West Bengal after the announcement of the 2026 Assembly election results. Meanwhile, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) has alleged unauthorized sorting of postal ballot covers and filed a complaint with the EC, while the EC has ordered repolling in the Falta seat on May 21 due to "severe electoral offences."

articles.title: Govt explores EU-like law to safeguard firms against third countries’ sanctions
articles.summary: The Indian government is exploring the possibility of enacting a law similar to the European Union's blocking statute to shield domestic companies from sanctions imposed by third countries. This move aims to safeguard Indian firms operating in global markets where they may face penalties due to geopolitical conflicts or trade disputes. The proposal is under consideration at the central government level, with potential implications for India's trade and foreign policy strategies.

This decision impacts how intermediary services are taxed under India's GST regime.

articles.title: Ease in Tax Litigation under Income-tax Act, 2025: A Structural Analysis of Procedural Reforms
articles.summary: The article analyzes proposed reforms to the Income-tax Act, 2025, aimed at simplifying tax litigation by merging multiple proceedings into a single unified process. This change seeks to reduce duplication and streamline procedures under the new tax regime. The reforms are structural and procedural, focusing on easing litigation rather than eliminating it entirely. The analysis is based on the government's initiative to modernize tax dispute resolution mechanisms.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns fragmented litigation under the earlier tax regime. The new law integrates multiple proceedings into a unified process, reducing duplication. The key takeaway is that litigation is streamlined rather than eliminated. The post Ease in Tax Litigation under Income-tax Act, 2025: A Structural Analysis of Procedural Reforms appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Additions Due to Lack of Corroborative Evidence from Third-Party Documents
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that additions to tax assessments cannot be justified solely based on entries in third-party seized documents without independent corroboration. The decision emphasizes that suspicion alone is insufficient to sustain tax additions. This ruling impacts how tax authorities assess evidence in disputes involving third-party documents.
articles.raw_summary: The case addressed whether entries in third-party seized documents can justify additions. The ITAT ruled they cannot without independent corroboration. It reinforces that suspicion alone cannot sustain tax additions. The post ITAT Deletes Additions Due to Lack of Corroborative Evidence from Third-Party Documents appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that a penalty imposed under Section 270A of the Income Tax Act for alleged misreporting is invalid because the tax authorities did not specify the exact clause of misreporting. The tribunal ordered the deletion of the penalty. The case involved a taxpayer challenging the penalty imposed by the tax department. This ruling sets a precedent for future tax penalty cases requiring clear charges of misreporting.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether penalty for misreporting can be imposed without specifying the applicable clause. The tribunal held such penalty invalid and ordered its deletion. This ruling impacts how tax authorities handle late GST revocation requests, emphasizing fairness over strict timelines.

articles.title: GST Parallel Proceedings Valid if Subject Matter Differs: Telangana HC
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ruled that GST parallel proceedings are valid if the subject matter differs, even for the same tax period. The court held that separate adjudication orders for short payment and excess Input Tax Credit (ITC) do not violate the bar on overlapping actions under GST law. This decision clarifies the legal framework for tax adjudication and may influence future GST enforcement and compliance practices.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether two adjudication orders for the same tax period constituted overlapping proceedings under GST law. The court held that proceedings on different issues—short payment vs. excess ITC—do not violate the bar on parallel actions. articles.summary: Mehraj Malik, a first-time legislator from Jammu & Kashmir, was released from detention after being the first MLA in the region to be held under the Public Safety Act (PSA). The PSA allows detention without trial for up to two years, raising concerns about civil liberties. Malik’s detention and release highlight debates over the use of preventive detention laws in India. The article quotes him stating, 'It irks some when common man enters politics…'.

articles.title: ITC Denial Upheld Due to Supplier Tax Default Under GST Law: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court upheld the denial of Input Tax Credit (ITC) to a purchaser when the supplier failed to deposit GST to the government. The ruling clarifies that ITC eligibility depends on the supplier's actual tax payment, not just the purchaser's compliance. This decision reinforces the government's stance on preventing tax evasion through ITC claims. The case involved ITC and highlights the legal consequences of supplier defaults under GST law.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether ITC can be denied when the supplier fails to deposit tax. It upheld the provision, ruling that ITC depends on actual tax payment to the Government. The key takeaway is that purchaser compliance alone is insufficient without supplier tax remittance. The post ITC Denial Upheld Due to Supplier Tax Default Under GST Law: Gujarat HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: TDS & TCS Changes from 1st April 2026
articles.summary: The Indian government is set to reform Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) and Tax Collected at Source (TCS) provisions starting April 1, 2026. The changes aim to consolidate TDS rules into a structured system and introduce digital compliance tools to improve clarity, reduce errors, and enhance reporting efficiency. The reform is expected to impact taxpayers and tax authorities by streamlining processes and ensuring better compliance.

articles.summary: The CESTAT tribunal remanded a Special Economic Zone (SEZ) refund claim after finding insufficient evidence to confirm whether the taxpayer had claimed both a refund and Cenvat credit for the same services. The case hinges on verifying that no dual benefits were availed. The decision impacts future SEZ refund eligibility and tax credit claims under India's indirect tax framework.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal remanded the case after finding insufficient evidence to prove that refund and Cenvat credit were not claimed on the same services. Refund eligibility will depend on verification of non-availment of dual benefits. articles.title: Govt mulls partial credit guarantee, interest subvention support for pvt sector electric bus, truck adoption
articles.summary: The Ministry of Heavy Industries is consulting stakeholders on providing partial credit guarantees and interest subsidies to make electric buses and trucks more affordable for private operators. These operators run 90% of India's buses but have been excluded from existing federal electric vehicle incentive schemes. The proposed support aims to accelerate the adoption of cleaner commercial vehicles in the private sector.
articles.raw_summary: The heavy industries ministry is consulting stakeholders on partial credit guarantees and interest subsidies to make electric buses and trucks affordable for private operators, who run 90% of the country's buses but have been excluded from existing federal electric vehicle incentive schemes.

articles.title: Why China Treats ‘Lying Flat’ as a National Security Threat
articles.summary: The article examines China's classification of 'lying flat'—a movement encouraging young people to reject overwork—as a national security threat. It highlights how the Chinese government frames opting out of economic struggle as a public offense, reflecting broader authoritarian policies on dissent and labor. The piece underscores the state's prioritization of productivity over individual freedoms, raising questions about governance and civil liberties in such systems.
articles.raw_summary: It's not about the economy at all: In a system that defines not struggling as a crime, opting out is a public offense.

articles.title: Wrong Section Claim Not Fatal: ITAT Remands Matter & Nullifies Penalty
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a genuine claim for capital gains exemption cannot be rejected solely because the wrong tax provision was cited. The decision clarifies that procedural errors in tax filings should not override substantive rights, setting a precedent for similar cases.

This decision impacts tax governance and compliance for eligible entities seeking exemptions.

articles.title: Routine BPO Comparable Excluded for Functional Difference Reason; Different Year Comparable Considered on Quarterly Data Basis
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a company providing high-end knowledge process outsourcing (KPO) and business solutions cannot be compared to a routine captive business process outsourcing (BPO) service provider due to functional differences. The tribunal also held that a comparable cannot be rejected solely because it follows a different financial year if reliable quarterly data is available. The decision impacts how companies are assessed for tax purposes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that a company engaged in high-end KPO and business solutions could not be compared with a routine captive BPO service provider. It also ruled that a comparable cannot be rejected solely for following a different financial year if reliable quarterly data is available. articles.summary: The High Court ruled that tax additions cannot be made solely based on a confession recorded during a search operation unless supported by corroborating evidence. The court emphasized that uncorroborated statements are insufficient to justify tax demands. This decision reinforces the need for robust evidence in tax enforcement actions.

articles.title: Consolidated GST Notices Valid as Law Does Not Restrict to Financial Year: Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that consolidated GST show-cause notices are valid under Sections 73 and 74 of the GST law, as they do not restrict the period to a financial year. The court clarified that such notices are permissible subject to limitation checks for each period. This decision impacts tax administration and compliance for businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that Sections 73 and 74 allow show cause notices for any period, not limited to a financial year. Consolidated notices are valid, subject to limitation checks for each period. articles.title: Bombay HC Allows SVLDR Benefit Where Liability Was Quantified Earlier
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that the SVLDR tax dispute resolution scheme must accept declarations where tax liability was quantified before the scheme's cutoff date, even if the declaration was initially rejected for lack of quantification. The court directed authorities to accept such declarations, clarifying the scheme's eligibility criteria. This ruling impacts taxpayers seeking relief under SVLDR by setting a precedent for liability quantification requirements.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was rejection of a declaration under the dispute resolution scheme citing lack of quantification. The Court held that liability was quantified before the cutoff date and directed acceptance of the declaration. articles.title: GST Penalty Order Invalid as Amount Exceeds SCN Proposal: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court invalidated a GST penalty order because the imposed penalty exceeded the amount proposed in the show cause notice (SCN). The court ruled that this violates Section 75(7) of the GST law and lacks jurisdiction. The decision sets a precedent for tax penalty imposition, ensuring adherence to procedural fairness in GST enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was imposition of a higher penalty than proposed in the show cause notice. The court set aside the order, holding it violates Section 75(7) and is without jurisdiction. This trend could strain local economies and social structures, potentially requiring government intervention or policy responses to address climate adaptation and rural distress.

articles.title: Why Russia's Northern Sea Route is a risk for global trade
articles.summary: Russia aims to develop the Northern Sea Route as a key global trade artery between Europe and Asia. However, the Arctic route faces significant political and environmental challenges. The development could disrupt existing trade patterns and energy supply chains, affecting countries like India that rely on maritime trade and energy imports.

articles.summary: Exit poll projections suggest the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) is leading in Kerala with 70–80 seats in the 140-member Assembly, potentially crossing the majority mark. The tight race indicates a possible shift in power dynamics that could impact future state policies.

articles.title: Mandatory Service Charge by Restaurant is Illegal: CCPA
articles.summary: The Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) ruled that restaurants charging mandatory service fees violates consumer rights. The authority found that automatic billing of such charges is coercive and an unfair trade practice, reinforcing that service charges must remain voluntary. The decision clarifies that default billing practices are illegal under consumer protection laws.
articles.raw_summary: The authority examined whether automatic service charges violate consumer rights. It ruled that default billing of such charges is coercive and constitutes an unfair trade practice. The case reinforces that service charges must remain voluntary. articles.title: Supreme Court Allows Appointment Of ReT Candidates In J&K Subject To Clearing TET In Three Years
articles.summary: The Supreme Court allowed the appointment of candidates from the Rehbar-e-Taleem (ReT) Scheme in Jammu and Kashmir, provided they clear the Teachers Eligibility Test (TET) within three years and three attempts. The Court directed the issuance of engagement orders, ensuring the scheme's closure does not retroactively deny appointments. The ruling involves Justices JK Maheshwari and Atul S. The decision impacts education policy and government appointments in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has allowed the appointment of candidates placed in select panels under the Rehbar-e-Taleem (ReT) Scheme in Jammu and Kashmir, holding that the closure of the scheme cannot retrospectively deprive them of appointment. The Court directed that such candidates be issued engagement orders, subject to acquiring the minimum qualifications, including passing the Teachers Eligibility Test (TET), within three years and three attempts. This order follows legal scrutiny of the recording's authenticity and potential implications for the state's governance and civil liberties.

articles.title: GST ITC Denial Due to NGTP Tags Hurts Genuine Buyers’ Rights
articles.summary: The article highlights a problem where genuine buyers are denied Input Tax Credit (ITC) under GST due to supplier tagging and analytics, specifically NGTP tags. This mechanical reliance on supplier data undermines fair tax adjudication and harms compliant taxpayers. The issue raises concerns about the effectiveness of current GST compliance mechanisms and may require policy or regulatory intervention to address unintended consequences.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns denial of ITC to bona fide buyers based solely on supplier tagging and analytics. The key takeaway is that mechanical reliance on NGTP tags undermines lawful adjudication and harms compliant taxpayers. The post GST ITC Denial Due to NGTP Tags Hurts Genuine Buyers’ Rights appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Karnataka court pauses stringent power grid penalties for renewable firms
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has temporarily halted new penalty rules imposed by the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) on solar and wind energy firms for deviations in power supply. The court stayed the implementation of these rules after challenges from the renewable energy industry, reverting to the old penalty mechanism until the next hearing. The state government has been directed to respond to the court by June 10. This decision impacts renewable energy firms operating in Karnata
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka court halts new CERC penalty rules for solar and wind deviations after industry challenge, old mechanism stays until next hearing, government to respond by June 10

articles.title: CBIC settles SEZ-to-DTA drawback dispute, treats re-exported goods as ‘imports’ under law
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has clarified that goods re-exported from Special Economic Zones (SEZs) to Domestic Tariff Areas (DTA) will be treated as 'imports' for duty drawback purposes under Section 74 of the law. This decision ends divergent practices and is expected to ease cash flow for exporters by standardizing the treatment of such transactions.

articles.title: Nomination Facility for shares of private companies held in physical form
articles.summary: The article explains a regulatory framework that allows shares of private companies held in physical form to be transferred directly to a nominee, bypassing the need for probate or succession certificates. This change aims to simplify and expedite the transmission of shares. The framework is relevant for private companies and their shareholders, ensuring faster and hassle-free share transfers. The post is sourced from TaxGuru, a platform covering tax and regulatory updates.
articles.raw_summary: The issue revolves around the complexities in transferring shares without nomination. The framework allows direct vesting of shares in the nominee, eliminating the need for probate or succession certificates. This ensures faster and hassle-free transmission. The post Nomination Facility for shares of private companies held in physical form appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: When GST Demand Goes Beyond SCN: A Practical Story from Telangana HC
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ruled that GST demands cannot exceed the scope of the show cause notice (SCN) issued to taxpayers. The court held that such expanded demands violate Section 75(7) of the GST law and must be corrected. This ruling impacts how tax authorities issue and enforce GST demands in India. The decision was made in response to a practical case involving GST demand procedures.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether GST demand can exceed the show cause notice. The court held that such expansion violates Section 75(7) and must be corrected. The post When GST Demand Goes Beyond SCN: A Practical Story from Telangana HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Drying Rubber Sheets In Smoke House Not 'Manufacture', No Panchayat Licence Needed: Kerala High Court
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court, in a recent decision, held that mere drying of latex sheet in a smoke house does not amount to 'manufacture' and therefore, the same does not require licence from the panchayat. articles.title: Generic PVC Fabric Description Not Misdeclaration; NIDB Value Rejection Quashed by CESTAT
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) quashed the rejection of a declared transaction value for Generic PVC Fabric by the National Import Data Base (NIDB) due to lack of evidence of misdeclaration. The tribunal ruled that transaction values cannot be rejected without reasonable doubt and supporting proof. This decision impacts customs valuation practices and tax policy enforcement in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that rejection of declared value without proof of inaccuracy was unjustified. articles.title: GST Registration Cancellation Invalid Due to Vague Notice & Missing Reasons: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court invalidated the retrospective cancellation of a GST registration due to a vague show cause notice lacking clear reasons and supporting documents. The court ruled that this violated natural justice principles and quashed the cancellation, directing fresh proceedings to be conducted as per legal requirements. The decision underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that a show cause notice lacking clear reasons and supporting documents violates natural justice. It quashed the retrospective cancellation, allowing fresh proceedings as per law. articles.title: From Access to Impact: Accelerating India’s First 1000 Days Agenda
GST Not Applicable on Arbitral Damages Without Supply Element: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that Goods and Services Tax (GST) is not applicable to arbitral damages or settlement terms unless there is a 'supply' element under GST law. The court quashed an Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) demand, clarifying that such damages do not constitute consideration for supply. The ruling impacts tax assessments for disputes resolved through arbitration in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether arbitral award damages and settlement terms constitute “supply” under GST law. The Court held they do not amount to consideration for supply, and quashed the IGST demand. The post GST Not Applicable on Arbitral Damages Without Supply Element: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.raw_summary: The justification for taxing alcohol as also for taxing it at a high rate has always been because of the ‘sin’ associated with it. articles.title: RBI accelerates gold repatriation as global trust wanes
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is bringing home nearly 77% of its gold reserves by March 2026. This significant move accelerates the onshoring of bullion. The central bank has already repatriated a large portion of its gold. This action follows global events that raised concerns about storing sovereign assets abroad. Many countries are now repatriating their gold.

Many countries are now repatriating their gold.

articles.title: GST Not Applicable on Solar Power Supply Due to Exemption for Electrical Energy: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that electricity supplied through solar power plants is exempt from GST, as electrical energy is considered an exempt good. The ruling clarifies that no GST registration is required when only exempt supplies are involved. This decision impacts businesses in the solar energy sector and aligns with existing tax exemptions for electricity.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerned GST liability on electricity supplied through a solar power plant. The AAR held that electrical energy is exempt from GST and no registration is required when only exempt goods are supplied. The post GST Not Applicable on Solar Power Supply Due to Exemption for Electrical Energy: AAR Tamilnadu appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Employee Transport Recovery Not Taxable as It Is Not in Course of Business: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) in Tamil Nadu ruled that employee transport recovery by employers is not taxable under GST because it is considered a welfare activity, not a business supply. The nominal amount recovered from employees for transport does not attract GST. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of employee welfare measures and impacts how businesses structure such arrangements.
articles.raw_summary: The authority held that arranging transport for employees is a welfare activity, not a business supply. Nominal recovery from employees does not attract GST. The post Employee Transport Recovery Not Taxable as It Is Not in Course of Business: AAR Tamilnadu appeared first on TaxGuru .

The move could ease oil supply disruptions but geopolitical tensions in the region remain a concern for global energy markets.

articles.title: Oil slips after Trump says US will help free ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices fell after U.S. President Trump announced efforts to free ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53 and U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10, though prices remained above $100 due to stalled peace negotiations. The situation highlights potential risks to global oil supply chains, which could impact India's energy imports and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices fell on Monday after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships in the Strait of Hormuz, yet remained above $100 due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10 amid stalled peace negotiations.

articles.title: First malaria drug for babies is approved in ‘major public health milestone’
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has prequalified Coartem Baby, the first malaria treatment approved for newborns under six months. Historically, there was no safe malaria treatment for this age group, despite high infection rates in parts of Africa. The approval aims to address the 610,000 malaria deaths reported in 2024, with significant implications for global and potentially Indian public health policies.
articles.raw_summary: WHO prequalification of Coartem Baby means newborns can be safely treated rather than using medication for older children The first malaria treatment for babies has been approved by the World Health Organization, opening the door to widespread use around the globe. In parts of Africa, up to 18% of children under six months will be infected with malaria, but there has historically been no safe treatment for the smallest of them. There were 610,000 deaths from malaria in 2024, about three quarters

articles.title: The Plant Fibre Sector Keeps Chasing Visibility When It Needs Markets
articles.summary: The plant fibre sector in India is gaining attention as industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact. However, output remains low due to fragmented supply chains, weak processing capacity, and uncertain demand. A FIBRAL report suggests that diversification will require standards, investment, and market architecture, not just increased fibre production. The sector's struggles highlight potential areas for policy intervention to boost sustainable alternatives.
articles.raw_summary: As industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact, alternative plant fibres are drawing new policy and commercial attention. Yet output remains marginal and forecast to contract, constrained by fragmented chains, weak processing capacity and uncertain offtake. A FIBRAL report argues diversification will depend on standards, investment and market architecture, not fibre discovery alone at scale.

This decision impacts property transactions where sale authority is disputed.

articles.title: Teen rape survivor’s choice vs unborn baby’s right to life—how SC’s new order deviates from precedent
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, rejected AIIMS' plea to deny a 15-year-old rape survivor the right to terminate her 27-week pregnancy. Earlier court rulings often blocked terminations if the fetus was healthy, but this order deviates from precedent. The decision highlights the conflict between a survivor's choice and the unborn baby's right to life. The case underscores the need for clearer legal frameworks on abortion rights in India.

articles.title: Private Premises Can’t Be Turned Into Unregulated Congregational Space: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that private premises cannot be used for unregulated religious gatherings if they affect public order. While private worship is permitted, organized gatherings are subject to legal restrictions. The court held that such use can be regulated under existing laws. This decision underscores the balance between religious freedom and public order in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved seeking permission for regular religious gatherings at private premises. The Court held that such use is not protected if it affects public order and can be regulated. The key takeaway is that private worship is allowed, but organized gatherings are subject to legal restrictions. articles.title: Sectoral Deployment of Bank Credit – March 2026
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released data showing that non-food bank credit grew by 15.9% year-on-year as of March 31, 2026, up from 10.9% in the previous year. The concerns highlight potential food security risks that may require policy interventions.

articles.title: US-Iran War LIVE: Trump says US will ‘guide’ stranded ships from Strait of Hormuz starting today
articles.summary: The United States will begin guiding stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting today, following reported attacks on two vessels near the waterway. President Donald Trump announced the move, which aims to ensure safe passage amid rising tensions between the US and Iran. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital route for global oil shipments, including those bound for India. The situation could impact India's energy imports and regional security dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: US Iran war LIVE: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to “guide” stranded ships out of the Iran-controlled Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two vessels near the waterway reported attacks.

articles.title: No Option for 18% GST as Outdoor Catering Falls Under Mandatory 5% Entry: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that outdoor catering services must be taxed at a mandatory 5% GST rate without input tax credit (ITC), leaving no option for taxpayers to choose the higher 18% rate. This decision clarifies the GST classification for outdoor catering, impacting businesses and tax compliance. The ruling was issued in response to a taxpayer query on whether concessional and standard GST rates could be selected.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether taxpayers could choose between concessional and standard GST rates. The AAR held that once classified as outdoor catering, the 5% rate without ITC is mandatory. articles.summary: The leather and footwear industry in India has requested duty exemptions on key inputs like synthetic leather, machinery, and chemicals, citing up to a 60% rise in costs due to the ongoing West Asia crisis. articles.summary: IndiaMART, an online B2B marketplace, stopped reporting traffic metrics in Q4FY26 due to a surge in bot traffic and a 1% drop in conversions after implementing OTP verification for unique business inquiries. The platform cited agentic traffic as the reason for discontinuing these metrics. The decision reflects challenges in maintaining authentic user engagement amid rising automated traffic.

articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced plans to guide ships safely through the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route controlled by Iran. Iran warned that any interference would violate a ceasefire. articles.title: Give our bullion back: India wants its gold under own lock and key
articles.summary: India is rapidly repatriating its gold reserves, bringing the majority home from overseas vaults. This strategic shift, driven by trust concerns and a desire for greater control, reflects a global trend of nations prioritizing physical sovereignty over foreign custody. The move ensures immediate access to wealth during economic uncertainty, redefining safety in international finance.

articles.title: Bombay HC allowed Depreciation on Leased Assets as Business Use Requirement Satisfied
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that leased assets qualify for depreciation under business use requirements, even if the lessee does not physically use them, as long as leasing generates business income. The court clarified that the primary condition is the generation of business income, not physical possession. This ruling impacts tax assessments for businesses engaged in leasing activities.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that leased assets qualify for depreciation since they are used in the course of business. It clarified that physical use by the assessee is not required if leasing generates business income. The post Bombay HC allowed Depreciation on Leased Assets as Business Use Requirement Satisfied appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Fan Drive Assembly Classifiable as Fluid Coupling Due to Functional Characteristics: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Advance Ruling Authority (AAR) in Tamil Nadu ruled that a fan drive assembly used in vehicle cooling systems should be classified as a fluid coupling under HSN 8483 due to its functional characteristics operating on viscous fluid principles. This classification impacts tax and trade policies for such components. The ruling was issued by a quasi-judicial body under India's indirect tax framework. The decision is binding on the applicant and has implications for similar products in the automot
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved classification of a fan drive assembly used in vehicle cooling systems. The AAR held that the product operates on viscous fluid principles and qualifies as a fluid coupling under HSN 8483. The post Fan Drive Assembly Classifiable as Fluid Coupling Due to Functional Characteristics: AAR Tamilnadu appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bombay HC says WhatsApp can’t wait for court orders to remove scam groups
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that WhatsApp cannot wait for court orders to remove scam groups from its platform. The court stated that intermediaries must act on complaints received through their grievance systems as per the IT Rules 2021, contrary to WhatsApp's argument that such decisions require a court order under Section 79 of the IT Act. The ruling highlights the legal obligations of digital platforms in addressing fraudulent content.
articles.raw_summary: While WhatsApp argued such decisions can be taken only by order under Section 79 of the IT Act, the Bombay HC said intermediaries are expected to act on complaints received through grievance systems as per the IT Rules 2021. articles.title: NDA leads in Puducherry exit polls; TVK emerges as key wildcard in tight race
articles.summary: Exit polls for the 2026 Puducherry assembly elections predict the NDA-led NRC+ coalition leading with 40% support, followed by the CONG+DMK alliance at 30% and the TVK+ at 17%. The TVK is seen as a wildcard factor, particularly among young urban voters. The results highlight a tight race with no single party securing a majority.
articles.raw_summary: Exit polls for Puducherry 2026 show NRC+ NDA leading with 40 percent, CONG+ DMK at 30, TVK+ at 17, Others 13, with TVK emerging as a key wildcard among young urban voters

articles.title: ‘Codeine cough syrups destroying generations,’ says Supreme Court, flags addiction among youth
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, hearing a case under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, expressed concern over the easy over-the-counter availability of codeine cough syrups, which are leading to addiction among youth. The apex court flagged the issue as destructive to generations and called for urgent measures to address the problem.
articles.raw_summary: Hearing a case under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, the apex court said that such syrups were being sold over the counter, making them easily accessible and leading to addiction.

articles.title: Mamata's missing ally: Why did Akhilesh Yadav stay off Bengal campaign trail?
articles.summary: Akhilesh Yadav, leader of the Samajwadi Party (SP), has not actively campaigned on the ground in West Bengal despite publicly supporting Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress (TMC). This absence has sparked speculation about SP's ties with the Congress and potential implications for the upcoming Uttar Pradesh (UP) state elections. The SP has clarified that its support for Mamata Banerjee remains firm.
articles.raw_summary: Akhilesh Yadav skips on-ground Bengal campaign despite backing Mamata Banerjee, sparking speculation over Congress ties and UP polls, SP insists his support remains firm

articles.title: Blast at railway track in Patiala was attempted detonation, killed operator: Punjab Police
articles.summary: A blast occurred on the dedicated freight corridor near Shambhu in Patiala, Punjab, which police have classified as an attempted detonation. The suspect died in the incident, and the railway track was damaged but later repaired. Authorities are investigating using forensic evidence and a recovered SIM card. The incident raises concerns about railway security and infrastructure safety.
articles.raw_summary: Blast on dedicated freight corridor near Shambhu in Patiala was an attempted detonation, suspect died, track damaged but repaired, police probing with SIM card and forensic evidence

articles.title: CERT-In’s first AI warning flags hacking, multi-stage attack risks from Mythos, GPT-5.5
articles.summary: India's cybersecurity agency CERT-In issued its first policy response on advanced AI systems, warning that frontier models like Mythos and GPT-5.5 could enable large-scale cyberattacks, including hacking at unprecedented speed and scale. The warning highlights risks of multi-stage attacks and underscores the need for regulatory oversight of AI technologies in the country.

articles.title: After Trump dismissed another proposal, Iran says ‘ball is in US’ court', ready for ‘negotiated settlement or war’
articles.summary: Iran has proposed a deal to the US that would reopen shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and end the US blockade, with talks on Iran's nuclear program to follow. However, US President Trump has dismissed this proposal. Iran states the ball is now in the US' court and is prepared for either a negotiated settlement or war. The situation could disrupt global oil supplies, affecting India's energy imports.
articles.raw_summary: An Iranian proposal that Trump has so far rejected would open shipping in the Hormuz & end the US blockade, leaving talks on Iran’s nuclear programme for later.

articles.title: I am one of Brij Bhushan's six victims, reveals wrestler Vinesh Phogat
articles.summary: Wrestler and legislator Vinesh Phogat revealed she is one of six accusers of former Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) chief Brij Bhushan Singh. Phogat raised concerns about fairness and potential bias in officiating during her return to competition in his stronghold, Gonda. She demanded transparency and accountability from the government regarding the allegations. The WFI president assured safety and denied any bias in the proceedings.

articles.title: GalaxEye adds to India’s remote sensing capability, launches world’s 1st OptoSAR Satellite
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based GalaxEye launched Drishti, India's largest privately developed Earth observation satellite and the world's first OptoSAR satellite. This innovative spacecraft combines optical and radar imaging, enabling it to 'see' through clouds and darkness, offering crucial capabilities for national security and disaster response.

articles.title: ‘He is a mouse': TMC's Kunal Ghosh slams 'Singham' cop Ajay Pal Sharma over repolling in Falta, demands suspension
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress leader Kunal Ghosh has accused IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma, nicknamed 'Singham', of involvement in election fraud during repolling in West Bengal's Falta area. Ghosh has demanded Sharma's suspension, alleging his actions contributed to rigging that has cast doubt on the election results. The controversy highlights concerns over electoral integrity and administrative accountability in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Following serious accusations of election fraud in West Bengal's Falta area, Trinamool Congress heavyweight Kunal Ghosh is demanding that the controversial IPS officer, Ajay Pal Sharma, popularly known as 'Singham', be suspended. Ghosh believes Sharma's actions are directly linked to the alleged rigging that has cast a shadow over the election results.

articles.title: 'All officers of TMC': BJP alleges irregularities after phone found in envelope near Asansol College strong room; chaos erupts
articles.summary: A switched-off mobile phone and documents linked to the Raniganj constituency were found in an envelope near the Asansol Engineering College strong room, sparking a commotion. A BJP leader alleged bias from officers towards the TMC and raised concerns over potential irregularities. The incident occurred amidst heavy security for EVMs ahead of tomorrow's results.

articles.title: Air India CEO race narrows to 4 names
articles.summary: The race for Air India's CEO position has narrowed to four candidates, including two Indian executives, Nipun Aggarwal and Vinod Kannan, and two expatriates. The airline's board is set to meet soon to make a decision amidst significant financial losses and operational challenges. The delay in appointing a successor to Campbell Wilson is reportedly adding to the airline's ongoing struggles.

The airline's board is set to meet soon to make a decision amidst significant financial losses and operational challenges. The delay in appointing a successor to Campbell Wilson is reportedly adding to the airline's ongoing struggles.

The tensions could escalate, potentially affecting regional security and global oil markets, which may influence India's foreign policy and energy strategies.
articles.raw_summary: The draft proposal was delivered to the US via mediator Pakistan on Thursday evening, the state media reported.

articles.title: Pakistan's Karachi faces severe water crisis as major supply lines burst after power failure
articles.summary: Pakistan's Karachi is facing a severe water crisis after major supply lines burst due to a power failure, leaving several neighborhoods without water for six days. Restoration is expected to take several more days. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in regional infrastructure and could impact cross-border environmental and water-sharing policies.
articles.raw_summary: Supply has been suspended in several neighbourhoods for six days, with full restoration expected to take several more.

articles.title: Bhagalpur bridge scare: Slab falls from Vikramshila Setu into Ganga River; no injuries, route diverted
articles.summary: A concrete slab between two pillars of the Vikramshila Setu (a bridge connecting Purvanchal and Seemanchal regions) collapsed into the Ganga River near Bhagalpur. No injuries were reported, and the route has been diverted. The incident raises concerns about the bridge's structural integrity and may prompt authorities to investigate or take corrective measures.
articles.raw_summary: A slab located between two pillars of the Vikramshila Setu, that connects the Purvanchal region to Seemanchal, suddenly collapsed in Bhagalpur.
